title: Fragrant Herbal Vietnamese Bread Canh Chua
description: A herbal and bouncy Vietnamese dish featuring bread and served in an agave leaf vessel, perfect for a post-workout meal. This recipe is FODMAP friendly and features a lamination puff technique.

ingredients.name: herbs
ingredients.notes: such as mint, basil, and cilantro

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the rice flour, coconut milk, herbs, agave nectar, sesame oil, and salt.
Add the active dry yeast to the warm water and let it sit for 5 minutes until it becomes frothy.
Add the yeast mixture to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rise in a warm place for 1 hour until it has doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a round loaf.
Place the loaf in an agave leaf vessel and bake for 25 minutes until it is golden brown.
Let the bread cool for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve the bread with a side of canh chua soup for a delicious and filling meal.
